preamble: Devoicing of the voiced alveolar affricate [dʒ] to the voiceless postalveolar affricate [tʃ] is a sound change involving loss of voicing while preserving the affricate articulation. This process is documented both as a historical sound change and in contemporary phonological variation.

examples.note: Devoicing of final affricates in certain phonological contexts, contributing to consonant cluster reduction.
Devoicing in the adaptation of English loanwords containing [dʒ], with assimilation to Polish phonological patterns.
Historical devoicing in the evolution of Old French affricates to Modern French consonantal inventory.
